title: Loss Functions & Training Objectives
summary: The loss function defines what 'correct' means. Cross-entropy for classification/generation, MSE for regression, contrastive for similarity, reconstruction for autoencoders. Choice of loss is an architectural decision — it determines the output layer shape and what the model optimizes for.

mathematical_core.cross_entropy: L = -Σ y_i log(q_i) — classification, language modeling
mathematical_core.kl_divergence: D_KL(p||q) = Σ p(x) log(p(x)/q(x)) — distribution matching
mathematical_core.connection: Cross-entropy minimization = forward KL divergence minimization (from FOUNDATIONS.INDUCTIVE_BIAS)

related: PRODUCTION.DISTILLATION — uses KL divergence on soft labels

notes: Custom loss functions for specific business objectives is a key skill for application design
